hook.text: Traditional roadmaps list features and dates. Outcome-based roadmaps list problems to solve and metrics to move.

buildup.text: Feature roadmap: 'Build dashboard in Q1, add filters in Q2.' Outcome roadmap: 'Improve activation from 30% to 40%.'

discovery.text: Outcome roadmaps give teams freedom to find the best solution instead of executing a pre-determined feature list.

twist.text: Stakeholders resist outcome roadmaps because they want certainty: 'Tell me exactly what you'll build.' Outcomes feel vague.

climax.text: The compromise: near-term items are specific features; far-term items are outcomes. Certainty decreases as you look further ahead.

punchline.text: Tell stakeholders what you'll achieve, not just what you'll build.

quiz.question: What advantage do outcome-based roadmaps offer?
options: They give teams freedom to find the best solution instead of executing pre-determined features
They eliminate the need for stakeholder communication
